reasoning: Panel 2-of-2 override of the live pipeline's dismissal, whose deciding premise misread the DAP spec. The declaration 'Safety: never allow terminate through DAP' (DotnetDebugAdapter.ts:617) is contradicted seven lines later by supportsTerminateRequest: true (:624): the snapshot's own docs/debugAdapterProtocol.json defines the terminate request as shutting down the DEBUGGEE gracefully ('does not directly affect the state of the debug session'), and the proxy plumbs terminate through (dap-proxy-message-parser.ts:47) with no gate on the capability. The stated invariant does not hold as written; fix is concrete (set supportsTerminateRequest:false to match the attach-safety posture, or scope the comment).
gray_reason: A line-scoped reading (the comment annotates only supportTerminateDebuggee, DAP's disconnect-time kill flag) makes it a sloppy but defensible paraphrase; the universal reading makes it false. Judges split on comment-scope conventions; panel severity readings also split (info vs no-downgrade for a false safety claim).
